name: Muralla China de Finestres – circular from el Puente de Penavera
name_original: Muralla China de Finestres – circular desde el Puente de Penavera
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Embassament de Canelles traverse a landscape characterized by tranquil emerald waters, towering rock formations, and unique geological features. Situated as a natural border between Huesca and Lleida in the Montsec mountain range, the region features the dramatic Mont-rebei Gorge and the distinctive Finestras Wall. This pre-Pyrenean setting offers a diverse environment for outdoor activities. Best easy hiking trails around Embassament de Canelles The most popular easy hiking route is Finestras Abandoned Village – Chinese Wall of Finestres loop from Finestres, a 2.2 miles (3.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the unique Finestras Wall, also known as the "Chinese Wall of Aragon." Another top favourite among local hikers is Chinese Wall of Finestres – Finestras Abandoned Village loop from El Milla, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the reservoir and the distinctive rock formations. Local hikers also love the Parking La Masieta – Congost de Mont-rebei loop from P 1, a 2.3 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through the dramatic Mont-rebei Gorge, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Hiking around Embassament de Canelles is defined by emerald waters, towering rock formations, and the Mont-rebei Gorge. The network offers options for various ability levels, including 16 easy, 37 moderate, and 24 difficult routes. The routes in Embassament de Canelles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Embassament de Canelles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Embassament de Canelles?
A: There are 16 easy hiking trails around Embassament de Canelles, offering a variety of experiences suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. In total, the region features 77 hiking routes across all difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes in Embassament de Canelles?
A: Easy hikes around Embassament de Canelles showcase tranquil emerald waters, towering rock formations, and unique geological features. You'll encounter dramatic landscapes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/494069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the distinctive Finestras Wall, often referred to as the "Chinese Wall of Aragon."

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy walks in the area?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village – Chinese Wall of Finestres loop from Finestres&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.5 km route that offers engaging views of the unique Finestras Wall, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks around Embassament de Canelles?
A: Yes, several easy circular walks are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinese Wall of Finestres – Finestras Abandoned Village loop from El Milla&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.4 km path that provides scenic views of the reservoir and distinctive rock formations, bringing you back to your starting point.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest along the easy trails?
A: Along the easy trails, you can explore impressive landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/494069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, with its paths carved into cliff sides. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3435367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of the Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7158840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penavera Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique perspectives of the landscape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy hiking in Embassament de Canelles?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning emerald waters, the dramatic rock formations, and the unique geological features like the Mont-rebei Gorge, which make for an unforgettable experience.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to viewpoints?
A: Many easy trails offer excellent viewpoints of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pantà d'Escales – la Pardina loop from Aparcament de la Pertusa 1&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas of Pantà d'Escales and the wider landscape.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on easy hikes in Embassament de Canelles?
A: Easy hikes in Embassament de Canelles generally feature moderate elevation gains, making them accessible. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking La Masieta – Congost de Mont-rebei loop from P 1&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 60 meters over its 3.8 km distance.

Q: Can I find easy trails that explore the Mont-rebei Gorge?
A: Yes, there are easy trails that allow you to experience parts of the dramatic Mont-rebei Gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking La Masieta – Congost de Mont-rebei loop from P 1&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.8 km easy trail that leads through this spectacular natural wonder.

Q: Are there any easy routes that pass by the Finestras Abandoned Village?
A: Yes, you can explore the Finestras Abandoned Village on an easy route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village loop from El Milla&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.1 km easy trail that takes you through this historic site.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking around Embassament de Canelles?
A: The region's pre-Pyrenean setting makes it enjoyable for hiking across various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be warm but ideal for combining hikes with water activities on the reservoir. Winter can also be beautiful, though some paths might require more caution.
tips: The Finestras leave you speechless. It's one of the most spectacular places. It is worth following the small path to the castle with hermitage.

Traditionally known as "Roques de la Vila", this unique geological formation was renamed the Chinese Wall of Finestres to encourage tourism in the area. You can access the foot of it by boat through the Canelles reservoir.
The abandoned village of Finestras is an awe-inspiring place where nature and man have combined their forces to create an unforgettable destination.
